login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Use to authenticate with Dropbox. Opens a browser for OAuth consent. Use --profile/-p to save to a named profile. Use --force/-F to re-authenticate if credentials are stale.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Use to clear stored credentials. Use --profile/-p to target a specific profile; otherwise clears the default.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Use to check if credentials are valid and see token expiry. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use --profile/-p to check a specific profile.

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: Use to manually refresh an expired OAuth token. Normally not needed — commands auto-refresh. Use when auth test shows expired token.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Use to verify credentials actually work by making a test API call. Use --verbose/-v for detailed check output. Good for diagnosing auth issues.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to see all configured profiles and which is default. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use --filter/-f to find specific profiles (e.g., --filter 'is_default:eq:True').

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Use to inspect a single profile's configuration. NAME is a required positional argument.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new named profile. NAME is a required positional argument. After creation, use `auth login --profile NAME` to configure credentials.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Use to switch the active default profile. All commands without --profile will use this profile. NAME is a required positional argument.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Use to permanently remove a profile and its credentials. NAME is required. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation prompt.

profiles.usage_instructions: Profile management for multi-account setups. Create named profiles, set defaults, and list/delete profiles. Each profile stores its own credentials.

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management — login, logout, check status, refresh tokens, and test credentials. All auth commands support --profile/-p for multi-account setups.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `dropbox cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `dropbox cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: path
arguments.help: Dropbox path to list (empty for root)

ls.usage_instructions: Quick file listing. PATH is optional — omit for root. Use --recursive/-R for subdirectories. Use --long/-l for size and modified date. Use --deleted/-d to include deleted files. For filtering/limit/properties support, use `files list` instead.

arguments.name: path
arguments.help: Dropbox path to list (empty for root)

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of results to return

list.usage_instructions: Full-featured file listing with filtering and pagination. Use --limit (default 50) to control result count. Use --filter/-f for field-based filtering (e.g., --filter 'name:contains:report'). Use --properties/-p to select specific fields. Prefer `ls` for quick checks; use `list` when you need filtering or pagination.

changes.help: Show files changed within a date/time range. Lists files modified between --after and --before timestamps. Uses server_modified time from Dropbox. Date formats: yesterday, yesterday 18:00, today, today 14:30, 2026-03-02, 2026-03-02 18:00, 18:00, 2026-03-02T18:00:00

arguments.name: path
arguments.help: Dropbox path to check (empty for root)

options.name: --after
options.help: Show files modified after this time (e.g. 'yesterday 18:00', '2026-03-02 18:00')

options.name: --before
options.help: Show files modified before this time

changes.usage_instructions: Use to find files modified in a time range. Requires --after/-a and/or --before/-b. Supports natural dates: 'yesterday', 'today', 'yesterday 18:00', ISO dates. Recursive by default; use --no-recursive to limit to one level. PATH is optional — omit for all files.

get.help: Get file/folder info by ID or download a file by path. When given an id: argument, returns metadata. When given a path, downloads the file to the local filesystem.

arguments.name: dropbox_path
arguments.help: Dropbox path or id: to get info/download

get.usage_instructions: Dual-purpose: pass `id:abc123` to get metadata, or a Dropbox path to download. LOCAL_PATH is optional — defaults to current directory. Use --table/-t for metadata display. See also: `files info` for metadata-only lookups by path.

put.usage_instructions: Use to upload a local file. LOCAL_PATH is required. DROPBOX_PATH is optional — if omitted, uses the filename in root. Use --overwrite/-o to replace an existing file; without it, Dropbox auto-renames on conflict.

rm.usage_instructions: Use to delete a file or folder from Dropbox. PATH is required. Use --force/-f to skip confirmation. Deleted items can be restored via `files restore` or `folders restore`.

mkdir.usage_instructions: Use to create a folder in Dropbox. PATH is required. Creates intermediate folders if needed.

mv.usage_instructions: Use to move or rename a file/folder. Both FROM_PATH and TO_PATH are required. Works for both files and folders.

cp.usage_instructions: Use to copy a file or folder. Both FROM_PATH and TO_PATH are required. Creates a full copy — not a reference.

search.usage_instructions: Use to find files by name or content. QUERY is required. Use --path/-p to scope search to a directory. Default max results is 100; adjust with --max/-m. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

info.help: Get detailed information about a file or folder.

arguments.name: path
arguments.help: Dropbox path to get info for

info.usage_instructions: Use to get metadata for a specific file or folder by path (size, modified date, content hash, etc.). PATH is required. Use --table/-t for formatted output. See also: `files get id:...` for lookup by ID.

history.help: Show version history for a file.
history.usage_instructions: Use to see previous versions of a file. PATH is required. Default shows 10 revisions; adjust with --limit/-l. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use the revision IDs from output with `files restore --rev`.

restore.help: Restore a file to a previous version. First use 'history' command to find the revision ID, then restore:
restore.usage_instructions: Use to restore a file to a previous version. PATH is required. --rev/-r is required — get the revision ID from `files history`. Use --force/-f to skip confirmation. Two-step workflow: first `files history <path>`, then `files restore <path> --rev <id>`.

files.usage_instructions: File and folder operations — list, upload, download, move, copy, delete, search, and view history. Use `ls` for quick listing or `list` for full filtering support. Paths are Dropbox paths starting with / (e.g., /Documents/report.pdf).

restore.help: Restore a deleted folder and all its contents. This command finds all deleted files within a folder and restores them to their last known state before deletion.
restore.usage_instructions: Use to recover a deleted folder and all its files. PATH is the original Dropbox path of the deleted folder. Use --force/-f to skip confirmation. Use --table/-t for results display. See also: `files restore` for single-file version restore.

folders.usage_instructions: Folder-specific operations. Currently provides folder restore for recovering deleted folders and all their contents.

info.usage_instructions: Use to see account details (name, email, account type, team info). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

usage.usage_instructions: Use to check storage quota and how much space is used. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

account.usage_instructions: Account information — view account details and disk usage/quota.

sharing.help: Work with shared links and folders

info.help: Get metadata for a shared link.
info.usage_instructions: Use to inspect a shared link's metadata (owner, permissions, expiry). URL is required. Use --path/-p to get info about a specific item within a shared folder. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

ls.help: List contents of a shared folder.
ls.usage_instructions: Use to list files inside a shared folder link. URL is required. Use --path/-p to navigate into subfolders. Use --long/-l for detailed info. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

download.help: Download all contents of a shared folder.

options.name: --path
options.help: Path within shared folder to download

download.usage_instructions: Use to download an entire shared folder to local disk. URL is required. LOCAL_PATH is optional — defaults to folder name in current directory. Use --path/-p to download only a subfolder. Use --quiet/-q to suppress progress output.

sharing.usage_instructions: Shared link and folder operations — inspect shared links, list shared folder contents, and download shared folders. All commands take a shared link URL as the first argument.

usage_instructions: Use for managing Dropbox files, folders, sharing, and account info. Commands follow `dropbox <group> <action>` pattern. Most list commands support --table/-t for table display, --filter/-f, --limit/-l, and --properties/-p. Use `files` group for file operations, `sharing` group for shared links.
